title: Parapolystichum effusum
intro: Parapolystichum effusum, also known as the mountain fern, is a species of fern belonging to the family Dryopteridaceae. The mountain fern has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in China, to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and inflammatory diseases, due to its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. Bioactive constituents of Parapolystichum effusum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include tea infusions and decoctions, which are often used to treat fever, sore throat, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Parapolystichum effusum is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This fern is native to New Zealand. It is a part of the genus Polystichum, but sometimes classified separately as Parapolystichum. The classification of Parapolystichum effusum can be complex due to ongoing taxonomic revisions. Some sources still consider it a part of the Polystichum genus.
